commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor revision plus real behavior proof; automation should not try to finish this external PR while the proof gate is missing and the security-boundary design needs maintainer review.; Needs attention: The diff touches the org namespace security boundary and leaves reserved-handle enforcement split across creation paths.; Review finding: [P1] Unify the org creation path — convex/publishers.ts:744-806 (sha=4237931f9382204d6ef6683b8eb6059df2710916)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a self-serve org publisher creation API and CLI command, schema/docs exports, tests, changelog coverage, and clearer scoped-package missing-publisher guidance.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the PR adds reserved-handle enforcement only in the new CLI/API helper while Settings continues to call the existing `createOrg` mutation.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor revision plus real behavior proof; automation should not try to finish this external PR while the proof gate is missing and the security-boundary design needs maintainer review.

target.title: feat: add self-serve publisher creation
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a self-serve ClawHub publisher creation flow so users can create an org publisher from the CLI/API before publishing scoped packages. This wires a new `POST /api/v1/publishers` endpoint, a `clawhub publisher create <handle>` CLI command, route/schema exports, docs, and changelog coverage.

Improves the scoped package publish failure for missing publishers. When a package such as `@opik/opik-openclaw` maps to a missing `@opik` publisher, the error now tells the user to create the matching publisher. For the legacy personal-owner case, it gives the full recovery sequence: create the org publisher, transfer the package, then rerun publish.

The publisher creation path creates an untrusted org publisher and makes the creator the `owner`. Settings and the CLI/API endpoint now share the same backend creation helper, so existing orgs, personal-publisher/user handle collisions, reserved public route handles, and reserved handles owned by someone else are enforced consistently across both entry points.

## Validation

- `bun run ci:static`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bun run --cwd packages/schema build`
- `bun run test convex/publishers.test.ts --testNamePattern "self-serve org publisher creation" --reporter verbose`
- `bunx vitest run convex/publishers.test.ts`
- `bun run test convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts --testNamePattern "publishers" --reporter verbose`
- `bun run test convex/packages.public.test.ts --testNamePattern "missing publishers|legacy personal|invalid ClawHub" --reporter verbose`
- `bun run --cwd packages/clawhub test src/cli/commands/publishers.test.ts --reporter verbose`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Notes

- `bunx convex codegen` was attempted, but this worktree has no `CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT` configured, so it could not run locally.
- Real CLI/API behavior proof is still pending; this update addresses the shared Settings/CLI/API creation boundary from review.
target.author: patrick-erichsen
